files_analyzed.target: Shimco North America Inc. and Shimco Aero Morocco SARLAU (aerospace manufacturing / precision shims)

files_analyzed.note: PDF extraction failed — file contains only '[ERROR] No PDF library available.' No usable FDD scope content could be extracted.

files_analyzed.document_type: Buy-side Integrated Due Diligence Scope of Work (Financial DD, Tax DD, IT & Cyber DD, HR DD)
files_analyzed.target: Diamond (plastics / resin-based consumer products manufacturing — B&T (US) Corp. and Polytainers, Inc.)

files_analyzed.document_type: Buy-side Financial Due Diligence Scope of Work (Financial DD, Tax DD, Optional Business Analysis)
files_analyzed.target: Sourdough (food manufacturing — bread, frozen dough rolls, garlic toast, cookies, muffins; brands include Redstone, Le Bon, Tasty Selection)

scope_periods.description: Fiscal years ended December 31, 2022 (FY22), 2023 (FY23), and interim 9-month period ended September 30, 2024 (YTD24). Also comments on FY24 forecast and FY25 budget assumptions.
Latest fiscal years ended December 31, 2022 (FY22), 2023 (FY23) and most recent trailing twelve months (TTM Mar-24 or latest sell-side roll forward made available).
Fiscal years ended March 31, 2022 (FY22), 2023 (FY23), 2024 (FY24) and most recent trailing twelve-month period ended (TTM Jun-24) or latest information made available.

phases.description: Sell-side FDD — full scope financial due diligence with no phase split
Buy-side FDD with core procedures ($275K–$325K) and optional operational cost and margin assessment ($45K–$60K). Also includes traffic light assessment of sell-side QofE at no cost (KPMG investment).

phases.phase: Financial Due Diligence + Optional Other Business Analysis
phases.description: Buy-side FDD core procedures. Optional in-depth revenue and gross margin sustainability assessment using transactional-level data.

bullets.text: Meet with the Company's officers and management in order to develop an understanding of the Company, including its:
History, organization structure, proposed transaction perimeter and management reporting relationships;
Start-up costs, capital expenditures, and status of operations in Morocco;
Intercompany transactions;
Production sites/ lines capacity, utilization rates;
Details of pricing methodology; key contractual terms with key customers (incl. ability to pass cost increases to customers);
Range of suppliers, significant dependencies; key contractual terms; price volatility for key materials; inventory levels and turnover;
Range of customer segments, customer concentration and regions served;
Key performance metrics used by the Company's management;
Employees compensation and benefits by major categories;
Financial reporting framework and information systems; monthly/annual cut-off process; and
Key accounting policies including: revenue recognition, inventory valuation methodologies, costing methodology, warranties, provisions, management estimates, etc.
Read available information and discuss with Target management in order to develop an understanding of Target, including its:
History, organization structure, proposed transaction perimeter and management reporting relationships;
Inter-group operations and allocation of corporate overheads;
Production lines capacity, utilization, scrap rates;
Range of products and services; degree of customization; pricing methodology (including volume discounts/ rebates); key contractual terms (incl. ability to pass cost increases to customers);
Research activities and new product development initiatives;
Range of suppliers, significant dependencies; key contractual terms; price volatility for key raw materials (resin, etc.);
Marketing and distribution channels;
Range of customer segments, customer concentration and regions served;
Key performance metrics used by Target's management;
Financial reporting framework and information systems;
Key accounting policies including: revenue recognition, inventory valuation methodologies, costing methodology, sales returns, rebates, provisions, management estimates, etc.
Obtain background information of Target, including its:
Organizational structure, ownership and management reporting relationships;
Range of products, customer segments and regions served; and
Financial reporting and information systems (including process, timing, structure of financial reporting, and interaction with the information/accounting systems).
Read Target's financial statements and discuss them with Target's management to gain an understanding of accounting policies and practices including:
Reporting methodology;
Revenue recognition policies;
Frequency of standard cost updates and procedures for analyzing variances;
Significant accounting estimates (e.g. bad debt provision, rebates, capitalized R&D, etc.); and
Recent or contemplated changes in accounting principles.

Based on the analysis and discussion with the sell-side due diligence advisor PwC, comment on Management's proposed adjustments to reported EBITDA and inquire about the nature and rationale of such adjustments and supporting documentation;
Perform a traffic light assessment of Management and sell-side proposed adjustments; and
Propose any significant adjustments identified that are not reflected in the sell-side Quality of Earnings analysis.

sections.heading: Critical analysis of the sell-side financial due diligence report (VDD Report)

bullets.text: Read the VDD Report and any available updates and discuss with EY and comment on significant areas including the following:
Management proposed adjustments to reported EBITDA (i.e. market rent, non-recurring items, customer chargebacks, contracted services , director fees, bad debt expenses etc.);
Completeness of the scope of work, any scope restrictions encountered, and out-of-scope areas;
Transaction perimeter;
Extent of access to management, quality of information available, and depth of analysis performed;
Procedures performed to form views on the appropriateness of Target's inventory valuation methodologies;
Views on the perceived accuracy of the monthly/annual cut-off process; and
Key financial risks and uncertainties with potential impact to value.

Summarize potential adjustments identified regarding the earnings of the Company in the form of a quality of earnings analysis, summarizing the risks that may impact revenue and/or earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization ("EBITDA") including:
Normalization adjustments identified by Management (i.e., Morocco start-up costs, FX gains and losses, non-recurring costs, etc.);
The impact of research and development expenditures;
The impact of provisions, management estimates, adjusting entries, or changes in accounting policies on reported results; and
Related party transactions and the pricing thereof.
Any other large, unusual or non-recurring events or transactions that may have distorted results and not identified by management in the financial statements; and
Other potential items identified during the due diligence process.

Summarize potential adjustments identified regarding the earnings of Target in the form of a quality of earnings analysis, summarizing the risks that may impact revenue and/or earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization ("EBITDA") including:
Normalization adjustments presented in the VDD Report;
The impact of any changes in accounting policies;
The impact of foreign exchange including constant currency analysis;
Related party transactions and the pricing thereof;

Recent changes to the business to understand the run-rate EBITDA impact (e.g. brand development and marketing expenses, business expansion into other product segments, impacts of capex investment etc.);

Any other large, unusual or non-recurring events or transactions that may have distorted results and not identified by management in the financial statements (e.g. non-recurring purchase orders; potential business disruption due to COVID-19); and
Other potential items identified during the due diligence process.
Identify potential adjustments to reported earnings before interest, income taxes, depreciation, and amortization ("EBITDA") by considering the impact of:
Management's proposed adjustments to reported EBITDA (e.g. out-of period inventory adjustments, non-recurring professional fees, Dot customer normalizations etc.);
The impact of acquisitions (e.g. Tasty Selections Inc);
The impact of supply chain disruptions, if any;
The timing of true-ups and other adjustments made less frequently than monthly;
The impact of any changes to senior management or team structure;
The impacts of foreign exchange including constant currency analysis;
The impact of commodity / raw material cost fluctuations and any associated hedging programs;
The impact of any key supplier cost arrangements including assessment of hedged vs. market prices and illustrative impacts to EBITDA;
The impact of Le Bon lease renewal;
The impact of related party transactions (i.e. non-fair market pricing, personal expenses, donations etc.); and
Other potential items discovered in the due diligence process.

Obtain a comprehensive view of revenue and margins. Obtain the underlying transactional data (i.e. sales database), if available, and gain an understanding of the major trends in revenue and margins addressing areas including:
Revenue and margin trends by customer, program, SKU, and product category:
Revenue and margin by significant customer and end market/ segment/ region;
Price and volume trends.
Discuss with Management:
impact of large or long-term orders/ seasonality (if applicable); and
any non-recurring components of revenue.
Breakdown of costs of goods sold including: materials, scrap/waste, labour expense, manufacturing overhead, packing and outbound freight for product sales; and
Ability to pass through changes in the cost of raw material and other inputs.

sections.heading: Revenue, value add and contribution margin trends

bullets.text: Obtain a comprehensive view of revenue, value add and contribution margin (pending data availability) by product line and by customer. Obtain the analysis and underlying transactional data (manufacturing orders database), if available, and gain an understanding of the major trends in revenue and margins addressing areas including:
Historical revenues (gross, net, volume), value add, contribution, average selling price and average cost per lbs by:
product line (e.g. by décor, technology, packaging format, resin type, category/ SKU grouping, sales channel, order size, if available); additional services (if applicable);
customer category (i.e. individual, business);
distribution channel (i.e. online, wholesale, etc.); and
price, volume, mix and cross-sell analysis.
Seasonal fluctuations in demand and monthly sales trends; and

Breakdown of cost of sales including: raw materials, labels (if applicable), scrap/waste/expired ingredients, packaging materials, freight-in, labour expense, manufacturing overhead, packing and outbound freight for product sales.
Obtain and read an analysis of Target's revenue and inquire about:
Gross to net revenue;
Revenue and gross margin by plant;
Revenue and gross margin by brand (Redstone, Le Bon, Tasty Selection etc.);
Revenue and gross margin by product category (bread, frozen dough rolls, garlic toast, cookies, muffins etc.);
Revenue and gross margin by customer;
Revenue and gross margin by SKU;
Organic growth and delineation between price vs. volume;
Pricing practices;
Promotions and discounts;
Any non-recurring components of revenue;
Revenue seasonality, if any;
Raw material costs including hedging programs and high level evaluation of embedded inventory gains and/or losses; and
Other components of cost of sales and key suppliers.
Obtain and read an analysis of Company's operating expenses and SG&A and inquire about and comment on:
Salaries and benefits (including headcount and average compensation costs, incentive-based compensation, severance, other), and any capitalization of costs;
Occupancy costs;
Repairs and maintenance;
General and administration costs;
Key suppliers and contractual arrangements;
Research and development activities and costs; and
Unusual and extraordinary items.
Obtain and read an analysis of Target's operating expenses and SG&A and inquire about and comment on:
Breakdown and historical trends of operating costs;
Personnel costs;
Occupancy costs;
Advertising, sales and marketing;
Professional fees;
Analysis of fixed and variable costs of the business; and
Unusual and extraordinary items.
Obtain and read an analysis of Target's operating expenses and inquire about:
High-level overview of employee compensation and benefits including:
Headcount and average compensation costs;
Accrued employee obligations and other provisions;
Severance / commitments, if any;
Incentive based compensation;
Flag any material gaps in headcount; and
Reconcile employee census to the financial statements.
Rent and logistics;
Food safety and quality costs;
Research and development costs;
Selling expenses;
Fixed vs. variable expense analysis; and
Other operating and G&A expenses.
Summarize and prepared normalized EBITDA bridges from:
FY22 to FY23;
YTD23 to YTD24;
YTD24 to FY24 forecast; and
FY24 forecast to FY25 budget.

sections.heading: FY24 forecast and FY25 budget analysis

bullets.text: Obtain and read the FY24 forecast and FY25 budget and:
Summarize the assumptions used by Management which underpin the achievement of the forecasts; and
Analyze the customer booking information to understand what portion of the FY24 outturn revenues (i.e. Sep'24 to Dec'24) and the FY25 budget revenues have been secured by customer orders.

sections.heading: Budget vs. actual results and outturn to latest forecast

bullets.text: Obtain the historical, latest interim financial information available and forecast for the rest of the year. Compare actual results to budget and forecast results to prior year.  Interview management about the reasons for significant fluctuations between periods and with budget. Discuss and bridge to full-year FY24 forecast with management. Comment on predictability of current year's performance and level of confidence in achieving the 2024 budget.
Summarize and normalize historical working capital in the business, including:
Comment on the historical working capital trends;
Understand key drivers and trade ratio trends (i.e. seasonality, days sales outstanding, days inventory outstanding, accounts payable days, etc.);
Accrued liabilities and prepaid expenses;
Large or unusual non-recurring items that may have impacted normal working capital trends including potential items identified within the quality of earnings analysis noted above;
Historical impact from acquisitions and associated normalizations; and
Recommend approaches to determine a normalized net working capital target/peg.
Summarize and normalize historical working capital in the business, including:
Comment on the historical working capital trends;
Understand key drivers and trade ratio trends (i.e. seasonality, days sales outstanding, days inventory outstanding, accounts payable days, etc.);
Accrued liabilities and prepaid expenses;
Payroll related funding and timing of cash flows; and
Large or unusual non-recurring items that may have impacted normal working capital trends including potential items identified within the quality of earnings analysis noted above.
Summarize and normalize historical working capital in the business including:
Commenting on the working capital trends, for the respective period;
Understanding key drivers (i.e., seasonality, accounts payable days, days sales outstanding, days inventory outstanding); and
Identifying large or unusual non-recurring items that may have impacted normal working capital trends including potential items identified within the quality of earnings analysis noted above.
Obtain and read an analysis of accounts receivable and inquire and comment on:
Trade and non-trade (due from officers, stockholder, employees, affiliates, and other) balances;
Aging analysis by key customer and by customer type and turnover analysis;
Credit terms and any other special terms; and
Allowance for uncollectible accounts and write-offs.
Obtain and read an analysis of Target's accounts receivable and inquire about:
Aging analysis and related credit terms (including special terms and changes thereto e.g. early payment discount elimination);
Trade and non-trade balances;
Allowance for uncollectible accounts and write-offs; and
Reserve and adjustments.

sections.heading: Accounts payable, accrued compensation and liabilities

bullets.text: Obtain and read an analysis of accounts payable and accrued compensation and liabilities and inquire about:
Accounts payable aging and days outstanding;

Accrued liabilities including (where possible) a basis for the adequacy of accruals for such items as bonuses, deferred compensation, severance, workers' compensation, commissions, vacation pay, litigation, customer returns, product liability, sales allowances and credits, and other;
Vendor/supplier settlement terms; and
Other current and non-current liabilities.
Obtain and read an analysis of Target's accounts payable and accrued liabilities and inquire about:
AP aging analysis and days outstanding;
Terms of trade with major vendors;
Payroll related funding and timing of cash flows;
Cadence and consistency of bonus and other incentive compensation; and
Other current and non-current liabilities.
Obtain and read an analysis of the Company's inventory and inquire and comment on:
Inventory costing methodology and its consistent and appropriate application during the historical period;
Inventory by category;
Inventory aging profile at each period-end date and historical write-offs;
Inventory turnover trends; and
Slow-moving, excess, and obsolete inventory and provisioning methodology.
Obtain and read an analysis of Target's inventory and inquire and comment on:
Accounting policies relating to tracking and controlling of inventory;
Inventory costing methodology and its consistent and appropriate application during the historical period;
Inventory aging profile at each period-end date;
Inventory turnover trends;
Key components of inventory and costs capitalized (i.e. product cost, labour, freight, other);
Slow-moving, excess, and obsolete inventory; and
Spoilage and reserve calculations, if any.
Obtain and read an analysis of Target's inventory and inquire about:
Composition by product category;
Inventory turnover by category;
Aging, slow-moving, excess and obsolete inventory;
Impairment and reserve calculations;
Consistency of accounting practices related to inventory valuation;
Purchasing procedures; and
Vendor rebates and allowances.
Fixed asset continuity schedule showing additions and depreciation;
Significant near-term required capex and deferred capex, if any; and
Historical capital expenditures segregated between growth and maintenance.
Fixed asset continuity schedule showing additions and depreciation;
Significant near-term require capex and deferred capex, if any; and
Historical capital expenditures segregated between growth and maintenance.
Obtain and read an analysis of Target's existing capital costs and inquire about:
Historical growth vs. maintenance capex;
If any deferred and planned capital expenditures; and
Impairment write-downs and issues.
Summarize and comment on net debt items presented by Management and other potential debt-like items; and
Consider whether elements of working capital have the nature of and may be reclassified as net debt, and if so, summarize the potential impact of these adjustments on working capital and net debt.
Summarize and comment on net debt items (on and off balance sheet) presented by management and other potential debt-like items; and
Consider whether elements of working capital have the nature of and may be reclassified as net debt, and if so, summarize the potential impact of these adjustments on working capital and net debt.
Summarize and comment on net debt items (on and off balance sheet) presented by management and other potential debt-like items; and
Consider whether elements of working capital have the nature of and may be reclassified as net debt, and if so, summarize the potential impact of these adjustments on working capital and net debt.
Inquire about significant commitments and contingent liabilities including:
Significant purchase commitments and product financing arrangements with customers and/or suppliers;
Operating leases;
Earn-out arrangements and commitments relating to previous acquisitions;
Incentive compensation and employee benefit obligations;
Expected or contingent liabilities (e.g. environmental, litigation, regulatory and tax); and
Pending or threatened litigation or investigations.
Inquire about significant commitments and contingent liabilities including:
Minimum purchase commitments under long-term supply and/or manufacturing contracts;
Incentive compensation and employee pension and post-retirement benefit obligations;
Lease obligations to related parties and 3rd parties (if applicable);
Pending or threatened litigation relating to infringement of intellectual property, regulatory, etc.; and
Other off-balance sheet transactions.
Inquire about significant commitments and contingent liabilities including:
Pending or threatened litigations by regulatory or other authorities and by employees;
Incentive compensation and employee benefit obligations; and
Significant lease and purchase obligations.
Obtain and read an analysis of Target's prepaid expenses and inquire about:
Composition of balances; and
Understand timing of cash flows vs. waterfall / timing of expense recognition.

sections.heading: High-level assessment of ASPE to IFRS / US GAAP

bullets.text: Review financial information and financial statements to gain an understanding of Target's key accounting policies and practices. Summarize any potential adjustments between ASPE and IFRS/US GAAP that may impact historical EBITDA or balance sheet accounts.
Inquire about and summarize related party transactions including:
Nature and extent of related party transactions; and
Basis of pricing for arrangements with related parties.
Review the overall appropriateness and sustainability of management's operating expenses and operating margin, especially critical factors leading to FY23 EBITDA margins;
Review margin contribution and operating leverage and determine whether costs are fixed, variable, or mixed;

Support assessment by performing external functional benchmarking of other players in the industry and related industries from publicly available information, KPMG's previous engagements and from KPMG proprietary Database;
Test the operational reasonability of management's cost forecast through the following:
Develop an independent bottom-up build of forecasted costs to test robustness, completeness and accuracy;
Develop a detailed headcount forecast in order to assess appropriateness of headcount to support revenue targets and identify gaps; and
Investigate the impact of operating leverage on the ability of the business to operate and sustainably support revenue growth.
Review of operational KPI trending;
If available, review profitability and contribution by product lines on a fully allocated basis, identifying performance considerations.
Review future opex savings overlays, including their sustainability and any potential cost headwinds not considered;
Review the forecast model and propose potential adjustments to how costs are scaled and adjusted based on findings; and
Provide a concise report outlining findings.

industry_specific_terms: décor / technology / packaging format / resin type
product category (bread, frozen dough rolls, garlic toast, cookies, muffins)
brand-level analysis (Redstone, Le Bon, Tasty Selection)
ASPE to IFRS / US GAAP conversion
inter-group operations and allocation of corporate overheads
minimum purchase commitments under long-term supply and/or manufacturing contracts

workstreams_found.note: Primary workstream in all three content-bearing files. Full scope extracted.
US tax DD with focus on C corporation entities (B&T (US) Corp., Polytainers, Inc.). Fee range $80K–$115K. Not extracted (non-FDD).
Canadian tax DD. Diamond fee range $40K–$60K. Not extracted (non-FDD).
Tax structuring at discounted hourly rates. Not extracted (non-FDD).
Canadian tax modelling assistance ($25K–$30K) and other tax advisory. Not extracted (non-FDD).
IT & Cyber DD fee range $70K–$90K. Not extracted (non-FDD).
HR DD fee range $50K–$70K. Not extracted (non-FDD).
Optional in-depth revenue and gross margin sustainability assessment. Separate from core FDD.

notes: Atlas Scope is a sell-side FDD for Shimco (aerospace precision shims manufacturing) — single phase, comprehensive scope covering two Canadian entities plus a Morocco subsidiary.

Project Diamond is a buy-side integrated DD engagement for a plastics/resin-based consumer products manufacturer — the most comprehensive SOW of the four files, with 6 workstreams (FDD, US Tax DD, Canadian Tax DD, Tax Structuring, IT & Cyber DD, HR DD).

Project Sourdough is a buy-side FDD for a food manufacturing company (bread, frozen dough, bakery products) — brands include Redstone, Le Bon, Tasty Selection. Includes optional other business analysis and Canadian tax DD.
EL Flag.json was extracted from a PDF but the extraction failed entirely — the file contains only '[ERROR] No PDF library available.' and no usable scope content could be recovered.

Manufacturing SOWs show distinctive emphasis on: production capacity/utilization analysis, inventory costing methodology, raw material/commodity cost pass-through, scrap/waste/spoilage, and plant-level analysis. These appear across multiple files and distinguish Manufacturing from other industries.

Two of the three content-bearing SOWs (Diamond, Sourdough) are buy-side engagements that include a VDD Report / sell-side QoE review step — this pattern of reviewing and critically assessing the sell-side advisor's work is a hallmark of buy-side Manufacturing DD.
Non-FDD workstreams were noted but not extracted per project rules. Diamond has the broadest workstream coverage (6 workstreams), reflecting the complexity of cross-border manufacturing acquisitions.
